summary: Set 10 Session 2 (3-session set, second non-final session): Authored the GitHub Actions release workflow + per-release runbook for publishing dabbler-ai-router to PyPI via OIDC trusted publishing, collapsed the README adoption section from the 60-line walkthrough to the spec's `pip install dabbler-ai-router + tuning + first set` shape, foreshadowed the Session 3 install command in the extension README's Requirements section, and tightened the pyproject.toml packaging so tests no longer ship in the wheel. Pre-session check confirmed `dabbler-ai-router` is available on PyPI (httpx GET against the JSON API returned 404). Deliverables: .github/workflows/release.yml (3 jobs: classify -> build -> publish-testpypi/publish-pypi; classify uses strict regexes to gate RC vs final tags; build verifies tag matches PEP 440 normalized pyproject version via packaging.version.Version; publish jobs gated by `is_rc`/`is_final` outputs and protected behind `pypi`/`testpypi` deployment environments; OIDC `id-token: write` only on the publish jobs); docs/planning/release-process.md (one-time PyPI/TestPyPI trusted-publisher setup, GitHub deployment-environment setup with required-reviewer guidance, RC dry-run section explaining the v0.1.0-rc1 git tag vs 0.1.0rc1 PEP 440 version asymmetry, per-release checklist, rollback path including PyPI's no-re-upload constraint and yank semantics, failure-modes table, maintenance section); README.md adoption section collapsed (was 60 lines, now ~25 lines: install command + tuning bullets + first-set bullet + editable/source-install fallback + forward-reference to Session 3's install command); tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/README.md Requirements section augmented with the v0.1 PyPI install path; pyproject.toml exclude/namespaces tightened (wheel went from 80 entries with 35 test files to 45 entries with 0 test files; package data preserved; entry_points unchanged); MANIFEST.in added for sdist parity. Verification: 2 rounds via gpt-5-4. Round 1 ISSUES_FOUND with 2 Major (broken tag/version check for RCs; over-permissive publish-pypi gate that could publish e.g. v1.0.0rc1) + 1 Minor (runbook didn't match the broken workflow); both Major issues addressed by introducing the classify job with strict regexes + PEP 440 Version comparison. Round 2 VERIFIED with one human-handoff follow-up (the publish path is authored correctly but not yet empirically proven until the human completes the trusted-publisher setup and pushes the first tag) and one trivial Minor (runbook hardcoded `CHANGELOG.md` in the commit example) which was fixed in-session. Cumulative routed cost this session: $0.289 ($0.1615 round 1 + $0.1279 round 2). Tests: 676 passed (no Python source touched this session). Human handoff for set close-out: the v0.1.0 tag push is operator-driven; Session 3 should be started either after the publish has landed OR with explicit acknowledgment that the install command can fall back to the GitHub sparse-checkout path until then.

reason.specifics: Session 3 is the largest single-session estimate in the set ($0.20-$0.35) and has the broadest TS/Python coupling (install command, router-config preservation logic, Provider Queues/Heartbeats graceful 'not configured' refactor, ~12-18 standalone-mocha tests, README screenshot-led collapse, VSIX rebuild). Opus high-effort matches the careful-wording demand on README + workflow paths.
